DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-22-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Demurrer to Amended Complaint.

No appearances are made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to the second amended complaint (SAC) is ordered off calendar for noncompliance with CCP 430.41. "If an amended complaint is filed, the responding party shall meet and confer again with the party who filed the amended pleading before filing a demurrer to the amended pleading." (Id.) Here, the SAC was filed July 27, 2021, and counsel's declaration indicates no meet and confer thereafter. (Layne Dec. 3:4-22.) Mr. Leipper is ordered to comply with CCP 430.41. Defendant's responsive pleading is now due by November 22, 2021.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR No. 5041, Email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-07-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
William Layne, Esq. (415-554-4217; william.layne@sfcityatty.org) Deputy City Attorney for Defendant, City and County of San Francisco.
No other appearances.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT:

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to the first amended complaint is sustained with leave to amend. The first amended complaint fails to state a cause of action and is uncertain. (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 430.10(e),(f). Plaintiff is granted 20 days leave to amend to file a second amended complaint that contains allegations sufficient to apprise the City of the claims against it, the alleged bases for those claims, and the relief sought.

Moving party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling via email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Eric Throne, CSR No. 7855, Email: eric.throne.csr@gmail.com, Tel: 707-305-8964. =(302/EPS)

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF IAN LEIPPER's Motion To Compel Discovery.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. No courtesy copies were provided. Continued on court's own motion to July 22, 2021 at 9:00 a.m. Per the instructions posted on the department's website(https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/civil/law-motion), parties must attach electronic copies of discovery motion papers to an email addressed to Discovery302@sftc.org. The subject line of the email should state the case name and number and the continued hearing date.
